evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS line + R²=0.877 annotation + OursModel highlight all present; fully satisfies Chinchilla-style scaling law brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set anywhere in script; default DejaVu Sans used throughout — text will be Type-3 bitmap in PDF.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4878CF) for models, red (#D65F5F) for OursModel — distinguishable under deuteranopia/protanopia, but not a formally CB-safe palette (e.g. Okabe-Ito); red used for emphasis not direction, which is acceptable.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,4.5) with tight_layout; annotation box top-right, legend bottom-left, OursModel label offset with arrow — no crowding or gaping whitespace evident.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure exactly (equation, R², OursModel coordinates); legend labels all three series; title present; no obvious Reviewer-2 complaints.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (21 KB, plausible vector size) but script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is embedded as Type-3 (non-selectable) fonts.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (21 KB), figure.png (57 KB), caption.tex (453 B), gen_figure.py (2 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the finding ('strong negative scaling trend'), gives the full OLS equation (y=−0.0449x+0.621), R²=0.877, and OursModel coordinates (log₁₀N=9.449, L=0.210) — specific and complete.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial, including a bonus SVG; the generation script is clean and self-contained.
Caption is publication-ready: it names the dataset size (80 models), states the OLS equation and R², and pinpoints the OursModel coordinates with precise values.
overall_weaknesses: Missing matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): text in both PDF and SVG will be Type-3 bitmap fonts, breaking copy-paste and downstream editing in Illustrator/Inkscape.

Default DejaVu Sans font is used with no explicit font family override; a publication-quality figure should specify a serif or sans-serif matching the journal template (e.g. Helvetica, Latin Modern Sans).
